label: 1689 CE
era_summary: 1689 (MDCLXXXIX) was a common year starting on Saturday of the Gregorian calendar and a common year starting on Tuesday of the Julian calendar, the 1689th year of the Common Era (CE) and Anno Domini (AD) designations, the 689th year of the 2nd millennium, the 89th year of the 17th century, and the 10th and last year of the 1680s decade. As of the start of 1689, the Gregorian calendar was 10 days ahead of the Julian calendar, which remained in localized use until 1923.

rome_italy.summary: Active conflicts in 1689: Tunis-Venice, 1682-92; Turkey, Hungary (until 1687), Transylvania-Austria, German states, Poland, Venice, Russia (from 1685), 1683-99. No Maddison GDP coverage for this region — economy held at neutral 50, reflecting the thin pre-modern record outside the European core. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).

andalusia_iberia.summary: Active conflicts in 1689: France, Savoy-Cologne, Austria, Prussia, German states, UK, UP, Spain, Savoy, 1688-97; Spain-Algiers, 1688-89. Maddison records GDP/capita 2114 (PRT, 1689) — used as the economy proxy for the whole region. Governance held at neutral (V-Dem coverage starts 1789).
